SnowDistribution: Firm snow. A bright start, then some summit cloud. Winds around 20 mph with significant windchill.
WeatherInfluences: Strong South-South-Easterly winds increase to gale force during the day. A band of snow overnight followed by further snow during the day which will gradually turn to rain at all elevations. Cold overnight with the freezing level rising above the summits in the afternoon.
SnowStability: Localised areas of unstable windslab will develop overnight and in the morning, mainly on West through North to East aspects above 900 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. Wet snow instabilities will develop in this windslab during the day as the temperature rises and snow turns to rain. The older snowpack will remain firm and stable. Freshly formed cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was a dry day with moderate Southerly winds. The freezing level rose to around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The older snowpack has a firm surface and good stability. Isolated and shallow areas of moderately bonded windslab are present, mainly on North to East aspects above 1100 metres with features such as gully tops and corrie rims most affected. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Diminished snowpack, good above 800 metres. Firm or icy underfoot.  Good visibility  Winds approx 10mph or less
Comment: Freezing level rising to above summits late morning.
WeatherInfluences: Some light snow showers overnight turning to rain by mid morning. Cold then the freezing level rising to well above summits during the afternoon. Moderate South South Easterly winds becoming strong by morning.
SnowStability: Some minor wet snow instability is possible late morning but only on some very steep North West to North East aspects above 950 metres. Elsewhere the firm snowpack will become moist after midday but remain well stabilised. All cornices will soften and weaken later. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Clear, dry and cold, though milder where sun exposed during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Good stability in all locations. The diminished snowpack is firm or hard in most locations though sun exposed areas were less unyielding later. Numerous cornices overhang very steep North to South East aspects and most remained frozen throughout the period except for those above sun affected East to South East aspects. Effective snow line around 850 metres today with larger expanses above this altitude. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: At higher levels, firm snow-ice on most aspects, beneath a thin cover of unconsolidated snow. Good visibility mostly sunny with cloud developing through the afternoon.   Winds approx 10mph or less, increasing 20 to 25 mph through the afternoon.
Comment: Very firm snow ice with serious run out potential on steep slopes.  Cornices will become more unstable through the afternoon as the freezing level rises.
WeatherInfluences: Strong Southerly winds will increase to Gale force during the day. Occasional light snow showers will become heavier during the morning before turning to light rain at all levels by the end of the period.  The 500 metre freezing level will steadily rise to 900 metres by dawn, then to above summit levels by the end of the day.
SnowStability: Localised accumulations of unstable windslab will develop on West through North to North-East aspects above 950 metres. The greatest instability will be on steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops. On these aspects as it turns milder wet snow surface instabilities will develop and remaining cornices which mostly overhang North-West to Northerly aspects will become unstable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Cool and sunny. Light Westerly winds backed to become fresh Southerlies. The freezing level rose to around 800 metres by midday.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a thin cover of unconsolidated snow on most aspects above 950 metres. The old snowpack has become very firm and is well bonded and stable throughout. Large cornices overhang some North-West to Northerly aspects.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Very firm patchy snow cover.  Cold clear conditions. Winds 10-20mph.
Comment: Snow will remain very firm with potential for serious consequences in the event of a trip or slip.
WeatherInfluences: Occasional showers will fall as snow above around 700 metres overnight and turn to rain at most levels during the day. The freezing level will gradually rise above all summits during the day with strong Southerly winds.
SnowStability: The remaining snow cover will be well bonded, very firm and stable. Any new snow deposits that fall overnight are not expected to be significant. While snow cover is patchy, significant areas will remain in gullies, corries and summit areas on many aspects over 750 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cold, dry sunny day with moderate mainly Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack is well bonded, very firm and stable. A superficial covering of new snow lies on some of the higher summits of the more inland mountains in the area. While snow cover is patchy, significant areas of very firm snow remain in some gullies corries and summit areas above 750 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Most continuous snow cover is above 800 metres which is firm and icy at all  elevations. Reasonable visibility with light to moderate South-Westerly winds 10-15 mph.
Comment: A slow thaw is expect, rain forecast amounts are generally fairly light.
WeatherInfluences: Overnight, mainly above 800 metres some light snow showers on moderate South-South-Easterly winds. During the day the freezing level will rise above the summits, rain and strong Southerly winds will then affect the area.
SnowStability: New snow amounts are not expected to be significant  and as milder conditions take affect some minor surface wet snow instabilities may occur. Steeper slopes, gully exits and steep corrie rims with a North-West through North to Easterly aspect will be most affected. The older icy snowpack will slowly soften and start to thaw and any remaining cornices prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After a cold night it dawned bright and clear with all summits cloud free with a frost in lower glens. Then some clouds started to appear with a brief snow shower the snow fall was short lived but the clouds lingered over the higher summits with some clearer spells. At first, freezing at levels with moderate South-Westerly winds the freezing level slowly rose through the day to around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Cold conditions have consolidated the snowpack further, it is frozen, firm and icy on all aspects and elevations. Deepest snow amounts are present on North-West through North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres other aspects will have lesser amounts. Very little snow cover below 700 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Firm snow - ice on most aspects. Good visibility. Winds approx 10 - 20 mph fresh, cooling.
Comment: Turning increasing mild through the day as the freezing level goes well above the summits.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will be around 600 metres overnight then rise throughout the daytime to be well above the summits by the end of the forecasting period. Fresh winds generally from a Southerly direction, later increasing to become very strong. There will be light precipitation which at first will fall as snow but then turn to rain at all elevations.
SnowStability: Isolated and shallow deposits of windslab will initially develop in steep wind sheltered areas on North-West to North-East aspects above 900 metres. As it turns mild there is potential for wet snow instabilities in the same places. Elsewhere the snowpack will be stable, with the snow being firm initially, turning softer later. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cold, dry and bright day. There had been an overnight frost in the glens, with the freezing level rising to around 750 metres during the day. Fresh Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is firm, icy and stable throughout. Coverage is good on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres and is extensive on the White Mounth. The avalanche hazard is Low.
